meaning: adj.小妖精的,象小妖精的n.小精灵,矮人,淘气鬼,小孩
examples: They were not elfin maidens, but mortal children.
Ellen \ 's raven hair curling around her tiny, elfin ears.
Beautiful scenery - knolls, elfin wood, pitted valleys resembling the surface of the Moon.
Scandinavian folklore is rife with tale of nisse or tomten, elfin spirits who haunt houses and play tricks on their occupants.
An elfin man with cropped black hair and a thin line of beard, he was dressed in the eccentrically antic manner of Raskolnikov.
